label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out what the author did when he met Mr. Lee. Let's read the passage again to make sure I understand. The author was walking with his mother and they met Mr. Lee. The author says he knew he could see Mr. Lee anytime in the neighborhood, so he didn't pay any attention. Then his mother stopped him and told him not to let that happen again because even a dog will wag its tail when it passes you. So the key here is that the author didn't pay attention to Mr. Lee, which means he didn't greet him. The mother's words were a lesson to always speak to people you meet, even if they're just in the neighborhood. Looking at the options: (A) He talked with somebody else. The passage doesn't mention him talking to someone else, just that he didn't pay attention to Mr. Lee. (B) He was attracted by a dog. The dog is part of the mother's analogy, not
